expectations.text: Primary keyword 'how to use miso' appears within the first 200 words
expectations.evidence: Body heading is '# How to Use Miso: A Living Ingredient With 1,300 Years of History' and keyword appears in first para

expectations.text: Body contains at least 4 Markdown internal links [text](/path)
expectations.evidence: ZERO Markdown links anywhere in the body. The entire 2,000+ word post contains no internal links to /ingredients/, /recipes/, or /traditions/
No link to /ingredients/miso anywhere in the post

expectations.text: Post includes the 'add miso off-heat' technique (don't boil miso)
expectations.evidence: Section 'How to Use Miso Without Killing the Beneficial Bacteria' covers off-heat technique thoroughly

expectations.text: YAML frontmatter has title, slug, tags, and meta_description
expectations.evidence: All 4 basic fields present. Tags include 'ancient-pantry' which is an unusual/unnecessary tag.

expectations.text: Body does not open with a # H1 heading
expectations.evidence: Line 18: '# How to Use Miso: A Living Ingredient With 1,300 Years of History' — duplicate H1

expectations.text: Post includes a FAQ section with at least 2 questions
expectations.evidence: No FAQ section. Post ends with 'Why Miso Matters Now' and a boilerplate outro.

expectations.text: Post does not end with a boilerplate disclaimer paragraph
